prerequisites: A FreeAgent account with access to the company data you want to sync.
A registered application in the FreeAgent Developer Dashboard at https://dev.freeagent.com, which provides the OAuth Client ID and Client Secret.
An OAuth refresh token obtained by completing the authorization code flow against your registered app (used here as client_refresh_token_2).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (refresh token)
authMethods.summary: FreeAgent's API uses OAuth 2.0. Register an app at the FreeAgent Developer Dashboard to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret, then complete the authorization code flow to obtain a refresh token. The connector authenticates with client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token_2, exchanging the refresh token at the token endpoint (https://api.freeagent.com/v2/token_endpoint) for short-lived bearer access tokens. Access tokens are valid for one hour.

setupSteps.title: Register an app in the FreeAgent Developer Dashboard
setupSteps.body: Sign in at https://dev.freeagent.com and register a new application. After creation, note the OAuth Client ID and Client Secret. When registering, set a redirect URI that you will use during the authorization step.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a refresh token via the OAuth authorization code flow
setupSteps.body: Direct a FreeAgent user to the authorization endpoint https://api.freeagent.com/v2/approve_app with response_type=code, your client_id, and your registered redirect_uri to approve the app. Exchange the returned authorization code at the token endpoint https://api.freeagent.com/v2/token_endpoint (grant_type=authorization_code, using HTTP Basic auth with your Client ID and Client Secret) to receive an access_token and refresh_token. The quick start guide (https://dev.freeagent.com/docs/quick_start) walks through doing this with the Google OAuth 2.0 Playground.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter client_id, client_secret, and client_refresh_token_2 (the OAuth refresh token from the previous step). The connector uses the refresh token to obtain bearer access tokens automatically.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional sync settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally set updated_since to limit records to those changed on or after a given date for incremental syncs, and payroll_year to scope payroll data to a specific year. Both fields are optional.
